title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer flagged three issues: (1) a `networkPolicy` doc row was added that belongs to a different PR (CC-0039) and should be removed from this one, (2) E2E Chainsaw tests are missing for the new `resources` feature and need to be added under `tests/e2e/keystone/resources/`, and (3) resource settings are not being propagated to Jobs.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review c13f89b with Claude CLI

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=0 critical=0 warning=3 info=2 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## WARNING (3)

### W-001: Scope creep: networkPolicy doc row belongs to CC-0039
**File**: `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md:116` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The diff adds a networkPolicy row to the KeystoneSpec table referencing CC-0039, not CC-0042. This row does not exist on main and was introduced in this PR, constituting scope creep.

**Code**:
```
| `networkPolicy` | [`*NetworkPolicySpec`](#networkpolicyspec) | No | `nil` | Network isolation configuration. When set, a NetworkPolicy is created restricting ingress and egress traffic. When removed, the NetworkPolicy is deleted (CC-0039). |
```

**Action Required**: Move this line to the CC-0039 PR/branch, or if CC-0039 is already merged and this was missed, create a separate doc-only commit.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Remove this line from this PR. Add it to the CC-0039 branch or create a dedicated doc-only commit/PR for it.
```

---

### W-002: Missing E2E tests for resources feature
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Test Coverage for New Code

**Problem**: The project has Chainsaw E2E tests for every existing feature (13 directories under tests/e2e/keystone/), but this PR adds no E2E test for the resources feature.

**Action Required**: Create tests/e2e/keystone/resources/ with a chainsaw-test.yaml that creates a Keystone CR, asserts the Deployment container has the defaulted resources, then patches spec.resources and asserts the Deployment updates.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Create tests/e2e/keystone/resources/ with a chainsaw-test.yaml covering: (1) CR with default resources produces a Deployment with expected container resources, (2) updating spec.resources triggers a Deployment update with new values.
```

---

### W-003: Resources not propagated to Jobs and CronJobs
**File**: `reconcile_deployment.go:163` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Kubernetes Resource Limits

**Problem**: spec.resources is wired only to the keystone-api container in buildKeystoneDeployment(). Three other pod-creating functions (buildBootstrapJob in reconcile_bootstrap.go, fernetRotationCronJob in reconcile_fernet.go, credentialRotationCronJob in reconcile_credential.go) have containers with no resource requirements. These Job/CronJob containers run as BestEffort QoS and will be the first evicted under node pressure.

**Code**:
```
Resources: containerResources(keystone),
```

**Action Required**: Either wire spec.resources (or a separate smaller set of defaults) to the Job/CronJob containers in a follow-up, or document this as a known limitation with a TODO referencing a tracking issue.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Wire spec.resources or dedicated defaults to Job/CronJob containers in reconcile_bootstrap.go, reconcile_fernet.go, and reconcile_credential.go, or create a tracking issue and add a TODO comment documenting this as a known limitation.
```

---

## INFO (2)

### I-001: resources: {} bypasses defaults, breaking HPA
**File**: `keystone_webhook.go:68` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: likely — **Pattern**: Enforce single normalization point for defaulted config values

**Problem**: The defaulting webhook checks obj.Spec.Resources == nil. If a user submits spec.resources: {}, it deserializes to a non-nil but empty ResourceRequirements. The webhook skips defaulting, the container gets no resource requirements, and the HPA cannot compute utilization ratios — the exact problem this PR aims to solve.

**Code**:
```
if obj.Spec.Resources == nil {
    obj.Spec.Resources = &corev1.ResourceRequirements{...}
}
```

**Action Required**: Also default when Resources is non-nil but has empty Requests AND Limits, or document that resources: {} is an explicit opt-out of resource defaults (and that this will break HPA).

**Suggested Fix**:
```
if obj.Spec.Resources == nil || (len(obj.Spec.Resources.Requests) == 0 && len(obj.Spec.Resources.Limits) == 0) {
```

---

### I-002: Default resource values hardcoded in five places
**File**: `keystone_webhook.go:69-77` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The default values (256Mi/512Mi memory, 100m/500m CPU) appear in five locations: keystone_webhook.go (defaulting code), keystone_webhook_test.go (test assertions), reconcile_deployment_test.go (fixture and test), integration_test.go (integration test), and keystone-crd.md (documentation). If the defaults change, all five locations must be updated.

**Code**:
```
obj.Spec.Resources = &corev1.ResourceRequirements{...}
```

**Action Required**: Consider extracting default resource values into named constants and referencing them from tests and documentation.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Extract default resource values into package-level constants (e.g. DefaultMemoryRequest, DefaultMemoryLimit, DefaultCPURequest, DefaultCPULimit) and reference them from the webhook and tests. Document that the source of truth is the constants.
```

**Related**: I-001

---

## Summary

A well-structured PR that cleanly adds the spec.resources field with proper defaulting, validation, nil-safety, and thorough unit/integration tests. The code demonstrates good separation of concerns with the containerResources() helper and includes comprehensive generated file updates and documentation. The two substantive issues are the missing Chainsaw E2E tests (breaking project convention) and Job/CronJob containers lacking resource requirements. A minor scope creep issue with the networkPolicy doc row should be removed.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| WARNING  | 3 |
| INFO     | 2 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Should not be merged as-is. Add Chainsaw E2E tests for the resources feature (F-001), remove the networkPolicy doc row scope creep, and track the Jobs/CronJobs resource gap (F-002) as a follow-up issue. The HPA edge case (F-003) and hardcoded defaults (F-004) are informational and can be addressed later.
resolution_summary: All five review findings were addressed: W-001 (scope creep) was fixed by removing the networkPolicy doc row from docs/reference/keystone-crd.md. W-002 (missing E2E tests) was fixed by creating tests/e2e/keystone/resources/ with a Chainsaw test covering default resource propagation and custom resource patching. W-003 (Jobs lacking resources) was addressed by adding TODO comments to reconcile_bootstrap.go, reconcile_credential.go, and reconcile_fernet.go documenting the known limitation and referencing the containerResources() pattern. I-001 (empty resources: {} bypass) was fixed by extending the defaulting webhook condition to also trigger when Resources is non-nil but both Requests and Limits are empty, with a new TestDefault_ResourcesSetWhenEmpty test. I-002 (hardcoded defaults in five places) was fixed by extracting default values into exported package-level constants (DefaultMemoryRequest, DefaultCPURequest, DefaultMemoryLimit, DefaultCPULimit) in keystone_webhook.go, and updating all test files (keystone_webhook_test.go, integration_test.go, reconcile_deployment_test.go) to reference these constants instead of hardcoded values.
